cards.meaning: once; in the event that (a hypothetical trigger with serious consequences)
cards.example: 一旦霍尔木兹海峡关闭，油价就会飞涨。
cards.examplePinyin: yídàn Huò'ěrmùzī Hǎixiá guānbì, yóujià jiù huì fēizhǎng
cards.exampleMeaning: Once the Strait of Hormuz closes, oil prices will soar.
cards.note: HSK-4 advanced connective; register leans 书面语/formal. Signature pattern: 一旦…就… ('once…, then…'). Near-synonym contrast vs 如果 (rúguǒ): 如果 is a neutral 'if'; 一旦 marks a threshold/tipping point whose consequences are serious and often irreversible — more written and more charged. (一 is pronounced yí here by tone sandhi before the 4th-tone 旦.)
cards.tiesTo: the Hormuz-closure tail risk after the Iran ceasefire collapse

cards.meaning: situation; state of affairs (usually political or military)
cards.example: 中东局势非常紧张，推高了油价和收益率。
cards.examplePinyin: Zhōngdōng júshì fēicháng jǐnzhāng, tuī gāole yóujià hé shōuyìlǜ
cards.exampleMeaning: The Middle East situation is very tense, pushing up oil prices and yields.
cards.note: HSK-4 abstract collocation, formal/written register. Collocations: 局势紧张 (a tense situation), 局势稳定, 局势升级 (an escalating situation). Near-synonym contrast vs 形势 (xíngshì): 局势 is a specific, often tense standoff — political or military (a war situation); 形势 is broader, the overall trend or circumstances (e.g. 经济形势, the economic situation).
cards.tiesTo: the Middle East situation driving the oil and yields reversal

cards.meaning: a threat; a looming menace or warning
cards.example: May banta ng digmaan sa Gitnang Silangan at banta rin ng malakas na bagyo.
cards.exampleMeaning: There's a threat of war in the Middle East and also the threat of a strong storm.
cards.note: Native-speaker contrast vs peligro (both often render as 'danger'): banta is the looming, approaching threat — a warning, something not here yet (banta ng bagyo, banta ng digmaan); peligro is the actual peril you are already in. Banta is what's coming; peligro is what's here.
cards.tiesTo: the Iran escalation and the approaching Super Typhoon Inday

cards.meaning: a lashing; the violent onslaught or battering of a storm
cards.example: Hinagupit ng bagyo at ng habagat ang kanlurang baybayin ng Luzon.
cards.exampleMeaning: The typhoon and the monsoon lashed the western coast of Luzon.
cards.note: Native-speaker nuance vs lakas (strength/force): lakas is neutral magnitude — just how strong; hagupit is the felt violent battering it delivers — the welt, the blow (ang latay, ang dagok). A storm isn't merely malakas; when it hinagupit, something got struck.
cards.tiesTo: Typhoon Inday and the enhanced habagat soaking western Luzon
